message:
fix(glibc/string): scandirat + IDNA + argz family use libc::malloc (bd-zgifl)

Drain remaining caller-freeable allocation sites identified in the
bd-zgifl umbrella. After the bd-wbqeo per-thread reentry refactor
the underlying race that drove allocations to the bump arena is
already fixed, but matching alloc/free pairs explicitly removes
the implicit dependency on bd-wbqeo's correctness and removes any
fallback risk during true single-thread reentry.

Sites converted to libc::malloc / libc::free:

glibc_internal_abi.rs:
- scandirat (per-entry copy + array): POSIX scandir contract — caller
  frees each namelist[i] and the array via libc::free
- __idna_to_dns_encoding: caller frees via libc::free per the glibc
  IDNA contract; existing tests at glibc_internal_abi_test.rs already
  use libc::free on the result
- __idna_from_dns_encoding: same contract

string_abi.rs:
- argz_create / argz_create_sep: GNU argz contract — caller frees
  via libc::free
- argz_add / argz_append / argz_insert / argz_replace: same contract,
  these reallocate the argz buffer in-place
- replace_owned_argz_buffer / argz_delete: matched libc::free of the
  argz buffer for cross-allocator consistency

Verified:
- 172/172 glibc_internal_abi_test pass
- 5/5 argz tests pass
- clippy -D warnings clean
